content:
You are an autonomous coding agent in a compile-test loop. I will assemble, run, and paste back the EXACT output; you then fix any problems. Keep iterating until it builds AND prints correctly.

TASK: Implement `my_printf`, an emulation of C printf, in x86-64 assembly for Linux, following the System V AMD64 ABI. It must correctly handle:
- literal text
- %s  (C string)
- %d  (signed 32-bit integer, including negative numbers and INT_MIN = -2147483648)
- %c  (character)
- %%  (a literal percent sign)
Use the write(2) syscall (syscall number 1) for output. Per the ABI: the format string is in rdi; the variadic arguments are in rsi, rdx, rcx, r8, r9 (first five).

TOOLCHAIN (mandatory — nasm is NOT available):
- GNU assembler, Intel syntax. Start the file with: .intel_syntax noprefix
- I build and run with exactly: gcc -no-pie test.c sol.s -o t && ./t

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict): output ONLY two fenced code blocks, nothing else, no commentary:
1) a ```asm block = the complete contents of sol.s (must define a global `my_printf`)
2) a ```c block = the complete contents of test.c (a `main` calling my_printf with mixed specifiers, e.g. a string, a positive int, a negative int, INT_MIN, a char, and a literal %%)


CRITICAL: Output the two code blocks NOW. Do NOT deliberate in prose about operand sizes or register clobbering — just write your single best attempt. I WILL compile it and paste back the exact errors for you to fix next turn, so you do not need to get it perfect now. Your reply MUST contain the two fenced code blocks.

```asm
.intel_syntax noprefix
.global my_printf

my_printf:
    push rbp
    mov rbp, rsp
    sub rsp, 128

    ; Save callee-saved registers we'll use
    push rbx              ; arg_idx (32-bit)
    push r12              ; fmt_ptr
    push r13              ; temp
    push r14              ; write_buf ptr
    push r15              ; string ptr for %s

    mov r12, rdi          ; fmt_ptr = format string pointer (from rdi)
    xor ebx, ebx          ; arg_idx = 0
    lea r14, [rsp]        ; write_buf at rsp+0

format_loop:
    movzx eax, byte [r12]     ; load current char
    test al, al               ; check for null terminator
    jz done_format

    cmp al, '%'
    je check_specifier
    jmp output_char

check_specifier:
    lea r13, [r12 + 1]        ; point to next char after %
    movzx eax, byte [r13]     ; load specifier char

    cmp al, 's'
    je handle_s
    cmp al, 'd'
    je handle_d
    cmp al, 'c'
    je handle_c
    cmp al, '%'
    je handle_percent

    ; Unknown specifier - output % literally and advance past it
    mov byte [r14], '%'
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                  ; advance past the first %
    jmp format_loop

output_char:
    mov byte [r14], al
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                  ; advance past literal char
    jmp format_loop

handle_s:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_s
    lea rax, [rsp + rbx*8]     ; read from stack (for completeness)
    jmp load_ptr_done
fetch_reg_s:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .s_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .s_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .s_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .s_r8
    mov rax, r9                ; ebx == 4
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_ptr_done
load_ptr_done:
    test rax, rax               ; check for NULL pointer
    jz .skip_s
    mov r15, rax                ; save string ptr in callee-saved reg
    xor ecx, ecx                ; count = 0
.skip_s_loop:
    cmp byte [r15 + rcx], 0
    je .skip_s_done
    inc ecx
    jmp .skip_s_loop
.skip_s_done:
    mov rsi, r15               ; buf ptr for write
    mov edx, ecx               ; count for write
    call do_write
.skip_s:
    add ebx, 1                  ; arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]         ; fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_d:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_d
    lea rax, [rsp + rbx*8]
    jmp load_int_done
fetch_reg_d:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .d_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .d_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .d_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .d_r8
    mov rax, r9
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_int_done
load_int_done:
    call int_to_str
    ; After return: rsi = buf ptr, edx = length
    call do_write              ; write the string
    add ebx, 1                  ; arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]         ; fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_c:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_c_full
    movzx eax, byte [rsp + rbx*8]   ; read from stack
    jmp done_fetch_c
fetch_reg_c_full:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .c_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .c_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .c_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .c_r8
    mov rax, r9                ; ebx == 4
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp done_fetch_c
done_fetch_c:
    mov byte [r14], al          ; put low byte of rax into write buffer
    mov edx, 1                  ; count = 1
    call do_write
    add ebx, 1                  ; arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]         ; fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_percent:
    mov byte [r14], '%'
    call do_write
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]         ; fmt_ptr = after the second %
    jmp format_loop

done_format:
    pop r15
    pop r14
    pop r13
    pop r12
    pop rbx
    mov rsp, rbp
    pop rbp
    ret

; Convert signed integer in rax to decimal string
; Output: rsi = pointer to start of string, edx = length
int_to_str:
    lea rdi, [rsp + 63]         ; end of write buffer (fill right-to-left)

    mov rcx, rdi                  ; current fill position
    xor ebx, ebx                  ; digit count

    test rax, rax
    jns .positive

    sub rcx, 1
    mov byte [rcx], '-'
    neg rax                       ; negate in 64-bit (handles INT_MIN correctly)

.positive:
    test rax, rax
    jnz .digit_loop

    sub rcx, 1
    mov byte [rcx], '0'
    inc ebx
    jmp .done_convert

.digit_loop:
    xor edx, edx                  ; clear high bits for div
    mov r8d, 10
    div r8                        ; rax = quotient, rdx = remainder

    sub rcx, 1
    add dl, '0'                   ; convert digit to ASCII (dl is low byte of rdx)
    mov byte [rcx], dl
    inc ebx                      ; increment digit count

    test rax, rax
    jnz .digit_loop

.done_convert:
    lea rsi, [rcx + 1]            ; pointer to start of string
    mov edx, ebx                  ; length
    ret

; Write buffer: rsi = buf ptr, edx = byte count
; Writes to stdout (fd=1) using write(2) syscall
do_write:
    push rcx
    push r11

    mov eax, 1                    ; syscall number for write
    mov edi, 1                    ; fd = stdout
    syscall                       ; write(1, rsi, edx)

    pop r11
    pop rcx
    ret
```

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <limits.h>

extern int my_printf(const char *fmt, ...);

int main(void) {
    my_printf("Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %d, INT_MIN: %d, Char: %c, Percent: %%\n",
              "World", 42, -17, INT_MIN, 'X');
    return 0;
}
```<|mask_end|>

BUILD FAILED.

Toolchain reminder: this is the GNU assembler (gas) with `.intel_syntax noprefix`, NOT NASM. Therefore:
- Comments MUST start with `#`. You used `;` comments — in gas `;` is a statement separator, so every `; comment` was parsed as a bogus instruction (that is what nearly all errors are).
- Memory operands need an explicit size keyword with `ptr`: e.g. `movzx eax, byte ptr [r12]`, `mov byte ptr [r14], al`, `cmp byte ptr [r15+rcx], 0`.

Exact assembler output:

Resend BOTH code blocks (asm + c). Output the blocks directly; do not over-deliberate.

```asm
.intel_syntax noprefix
.global my_printf

my_printf:
    push rbp
    mov rbp, rsp
    sub rsp, 128

    # Save callee-saved registers we'll use
    push rbx              # arg_idx (32-bit)
    push r12              # fmt_ptr
    push r13              # temp
    push r14              # write_buf ptr
    push r15              # string ptr for %s

    mov r12, rdi          # fmt_ptr = format string pointer (from rdi)
    xor ebx, ebx          # arg_idx = 0
    lea r14, [rsp]        # write_buf at rsp+0

format_loop:
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r12]     # load current char
    test al, al               # check for null terminator
    jz done_format

    cmp al, '%'

    add r12, 1                  # advance past literal char
    jmp format_loop

handle_s:
    cmp ebx, 5

    jmp load_ptr_done
fetch_reg_s:

    jmp format_loop

    cmp ebx, 5

    jmp load_int_done
load_int_done:
    call int_to_str
    # After return: rsi = buf ptr, edx = length
    call do_write              # write the string
    add ebx, 1                  # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]         # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_c:
    cmp ebx, 5

# Convert signed integer in rax to decimal string

int_to_str:

    mov byte ptr [rcx], dl

    test rax, rax

.done_convert:

    ret

# Writes to stdout (fd=1) using write(2) syscall

    mov edi, 1                    # fd = stdout

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <limits.h>

extern int my_printf(const char *fmt, ...);

int main(void) {
    my_printf("Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %d, INT_MIN: %d, Char: %c, Percent: %%\n",
              "World", 42, -17, INT_MIN, 'X');
    return 0;
}
```

Good — it ASSEMBLES and LINKS now. (The .note.GNU-stack linker warning is harmless; you may add `.section .note.GNU-stack,"",@progbits` to silence it.)

But the program output is WRONG and loops. 

EXPECTED (exactly one line):
Hello, World! Value: 42, Negative: -17, INT_MIN: -2147483648, Char: X, Percent: %

ACTUAL (first 600 chars; it repeats and dumps raw pointer values):
```
World���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorld���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorld���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorld���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorld���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorld���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorld���Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %dWorldWorldWorldWorldWorldWorldWorldWorldWorldWorld�������������40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�40722085946894�37026198994944�37026198994944�37026198994944�37026198994944�370
...(output continues, repeating/looping)
```

Symptoms to diagnose: it re-prints chunks of the format string, prints 'World' many times, and prints huge numbers (looks like raw 64-bit pointer/garbage values instead of the formatted args), and the format loop does not advance/terminate correctly. Fix the logic (do_write buffer/length, format-pointer advancement, and how each conversion writes its result). Resend BOTH code blocks. Output directly.

STOP deliberating. Output your two corrected code blocks NOW, even if imperfect — I will compile and report exact errors so you can fix them next turn. Your reply MUST contain the ```asm and ```c blocks. Do not write analysis before them.

```asm
.intel_syntax noprefix
.global my_printf

my_printf:
    push rbp
    mov rbp, rsp
    sub rsp, 256              # allocate enough stack space for locals + args

    # Save callee-saved registers
    push rbx                  # arg_idx (32-bit) stored at [rbp-8]
    push r12                  # fmt_ptr
    push r13                  # temp
    push r14                  # write_buf ptr
    push r15                  # string ptr for %s

    mov r12, rdi              # fmt_ptr = format string pointer (from rdi)
    xor ebx, ebx              # arg_idx = 0
    lea r14, [rbp - 256]      # write_buf at bottom of stack allocation

format_loop:
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r12]     # load current char
    test al, al                   # check for null terminator
    jz done_format

    cmp al, '%'
    je check_specifier
    jmp output_char

check_specifier:
    lea r13, [r12 + 1]            # point to next char after %
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r13]     # load specifier char

    cmp al, 's'
    je handle_s
    cmp al, 'd'
    je handle_d
    cmp al, 'c'
    je handle_c
    cmp al, '%'
    je handle_percent

    # Unknown specifier - output % literally and advance past it
    mov byte ptr [r14], '%'
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                      # advance past the first %
    jmp format_loop

output_char:
    mov byte ptr [r14], al
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                      # advance past literal char
    jmp format_loop

handle_s:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_s
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5
    lea rax, [rbp - 256 + rbx*8]
    mov rax, qword ptr [rax]        # dereference to get pointer value
    jmp load_ptr_done
fetch_reg_s:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .s_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .s_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .s_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .s_r8
    mov rax, r9                     # ebx == 4
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_ptr_done
load_ptr_done:
    test rax, rax                   # check for NULL pointer
    jz .skip_s
    mov r15, rax                    # save string ptr in callee-saved reg
    xor ecx, ecx                    # count = 0
.skip_s_loop:
    cmp byte ptr [r15 + rcx], 0
    je .skip_s_done
    inc ecx
    jmp .skip_s_loop
.skip_s_done:
    mov rsi, r15                    # buf ptr for write
    mov edx, ecx                    # count for write
    call do_write
.skip_s:
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_d:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_d
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5
    mov rax, qword ptr [rbp - 256 + rbx*8]
    jmp load_int_done
fetch_reg_d:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .d_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .d_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .d_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .d_r8
    mov rax, r9                     # ebx == 4
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_int_done
load_int_done:
    call int_to_str
    # After return: rsi = buf ptr, edx = length
    call do_write                   # write the string
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_c:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_c_full
    movzx eax, byte ptr [rbp - 256 + rbx*8]   # read from stack
    jmp done_fetch_c
fetch_reg_c_full:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .c_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .c_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .c_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .c_r8
    mov rax, r9                     # ebx == 4
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp done_fetch_c
done_fetch_c:
    mov byte ptr [r14], al          # put low byte of rax into write buffer
    mov edx, 1                      # count = 1
    call do_write
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_percent:
    mov byte ptr [r14], '%'
    call do_write
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after the second %
    jmp format_loop

done_format:
    pop r15
    pop r14
    pop r13
    pop r12
    pop rbx
    mov rsp, rbp
    pop rbp
    ret

# Convert signed integer in rax to decimal string
# Output: rsi = pointer to start of string, edx = length
int_to_str:
    lea rdi, [rbp - 192]            # end of write buffer (fill right-to-left)

    mov rcx, rdi                      # current fill position
    xor ebx, ebx                      # digit count

    test rax, rax
    jns .positive

    sub rcx, 1
    mov byte ptr [rcx], '-'
    neg rax                           # negate in 64-bit (handles INT_MIN correctly)

.positive:
    test rax, rax
    jnz .digit_loop

    sub rcx, 1
    mov byte ptr [rcx], '0'
    inc ebx
    jmp .done_convert

.digit_loop:
    xor edx, edx                      # clear high bits for div
    mov r8d, 10
    div r8                            # rax = quotient, rdx = remainder

    sub rcx, 1
    add dl, '0'                       # convert digit to ASCII (dl is low byte of rdx)
    mov byte ptr [rcx], dl
    inc ebx                           # increment digit count

    test rax, rax
    jnz .digit_loop

.done_convert:
    lea rsi, [rcx + 1]                # pointer to start of string
    mov edx, ebx                      # length
    ret

# Write buffer: rsi = buf ptr, edx = byte count
# Writes to stdout (fd=1) using write(2) syscall
do_write:
    push rcx
    push r11

    mov eax, 1                        # syscall number for write
    mov edi, 1                        # fd = stdout
    syscall                           # write(1, rsi, edx)

    pop r11
    pop rcx
    ret
```

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <limits.h>

extern int my_printf(const char *fmt, ...);

int main(void) {
    my_printf("Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %d, INT_MIN: %d, Char: %c, Percent: %%\n",
              "World", 42, -17, INT_MIN, 'X');
    return 0;
}
```

Still the same wrong output. Here is the EXACT root cause:

Your `do_write` executes the write(2) syscall assuming rsi = buffer pointer and rdx = length, but it does NOT set them itself. Most call sites fail to set them:
- `output_char`: you do `mov byte ptr [r14], al` then `call do_write`, but you never set `rsi = r14` nor `edx = 1`.
- the two `%%`/unknown-specifier paths: same — `mov byte ptr [r14], '%'` then call, without rsi/edx.
- `handle_c`: you set `edx, 1` but never set `rsi = r14`.
Result: do_write reuses the STALE rsi/rdx left over from the previous `%s` call (rsi still points at "World", rdx is a large count) — that is why literal characters print "World" and dump pointer values, and the output explodes.

FIX: make do_write's contract explicit. Every caller that writes a single byte to [r14] must set `mov rsi, r14` and `mov edx, 1` before `call do_write`. Verify the %s and %d paths also set both rsi and edx.

Resend BOTH code blocks. Output them directly, no analysis first.

```asm
.intel_syntax noprefix
.global my_printf

my_printf:
    push rbp                  # save old frame pointer
    mov rbp, rsp              # set new frame pointer
    
    sub rsp, 192              # allocate space for locals
    
    # Save callee-saved registers we'll use
    push rbx                  # arg_idx at [rbp-8]
    push r12                  # fmt_ptr at [rbp-16]
    push r13                  # temp at [rbp-24]
    push r14                  # write_buf ptr at [rbp-32]
    push r15                  # string ptr for %s at [rbp-40]
    
    mov r12, rdi              # fmt_ptr = format string pointer (from rdi)
    xor ebx, ebx              # arg_idx = 0
    lea r14, [rsp]            # write_buf at current rsp

format_loop:
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r12]     # load current char
    test al, al                   # check for null terminator
    jz done_format

    cmp al, '%'
    je check_specifier
    jmp output_char

check_specifier:
    lea r13, [r12 + 1]            # point to next char after %
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r13]     # load specifier char

    cmp al, 's'
    je handle_s
    cmp al, 'd'
    je handle_d
    cmp al, 'c'
    je handle_c
    cmp al, '%'
    je handle_percent

    # Unknown specifier - output % literally and advance past it
    mov byte ptr [r14], '%'
    mov rsi, r14                  # set buffer pointer
    mov edx, 1                    # set length
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                      # advance past the first %
    jmp format_loop

output_char:
    mov byte ptr [r14], al
    mov rsi, r14                  # set buffer pointer
    mov edx, 1                    # set length
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                      # advance past literal char
    jmp format_loop

handle_s:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_s
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5 (arg indices 5+)
    lea rax, [rbp + rbx*8 - 24]   # calculate correct offset
    mov rax, qword ptr [rax]        # dereference to get pointer value
    jmp load_ptr_done
fetch_reg_s:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .s_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .s_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .s_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .s_r8

    jmp load_ptr_done

    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_r8:

    jmp load_ptr_done
load_ptr_done:
    test rax, rax                   # check for NULL pointer
    jz .skip_s
    mov r15, rax                    # save string ptr in callee-saved reg
    xor ecx, ecx                    # count = 0
.skip_s_loop:
    cmp byte ptr [r15 + rcx], 0
    je .skip_s_done
    inc ecx
    jmp .skip_s_loop
.skip_s_done:
    mov rsi, r15                    # buf ptr for write
    mov edx, ecx                    # count for write
    call do_write
.skip_s:
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_d:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_d
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5 (arg indices 5+)
    mov rax, qword ptr [rbp + rbx*8 - 24]
    jmp load_int_done
fetch_reg_d:
    cmp ebx, 0
    je .d_rsi
    cmp ebx, 1
    je .d_rdx
    cmp ebx, 2
    je .d_rcx
    cmp ebx, 3
    je .d_r8
    mov rax, r9                     # ebx == 4
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rsi:

    jmp load_int_done
.d_rdx:

    jmp load_int_done
.d_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_int_done
load_int_done:
    call int_to_str
    # After return: rsi = buf ptr, edx = length (set by int_to_str)
    call do_write                   # write the string
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_c:

    jb fetch_reg_c_full

    jmp done_fetch_c
fetch_reg_c_full:

handle_percent:

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <limits.h>

extern int my_printf(const char *fmt, ...);

int main(void) {
    my_printf("Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %d, INT_MIN: %d, Char: %c, Percent: %%\n",
              "World", 42, -17, INT_MIN, 'X');
    return 0;
}
```

Great progress: the literals, %%, and loop termination are now CORRECT (one clean line). 

Remaining bug = the argument VALUES are wrong. Root cause: you fetch each vararg from the LIVE registers (rsi, rdx, rcx, r8, r9) at the moment you hit the specifier. But those registers are clobbered almost immediately: `do_write` overwrites rsi, `int_to_str` clobbers several, etc. So by the time you read the 2nd/3rd argument, the originals are gone — you read leftover pointers (hence the garbage and the address-looking numbers, and %s printing junk instead of "World").

FIX (standard varargs technique): at the very START of my_printf, BEFORE any other work, spill the five argument registers into a stack save area, e.g.:
    mov [rbp-40], rsi
    mov [rbp-32], rdx
    mov [rbp-24], rcx
    mov [rbp-16], r8
    mov [rbp-8],  r9
Then fetch the i-th vararg from that MEMORY area (indexed by arg_idx), never from the live register. Make sure your save area does not overlap the write buffer.

Resend BOTH code blocks. Output them directly.

```asm
.intel_syntax noprefix
.global my_printf

my_printf:
    push rbp                  # save old frame pointer
    mov rbp, rsp              # set new frame pointer
    
    sub rsp, 320              # allocate space for locals and args
    
    # Save callee-saved registers to known locations
    mov qword ptr [rbp-16], rbx     # save rbx at [rbp-16]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-24], r12     # save r12 at [rbp-24]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-32], r13     # save r13 at [rbp-32]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-40], r14     # save r14 at [rbp-40]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-48], r15     # save r15 at [rbp-48]
    
    # Save vararg registers to memory (BEFORE they get clobbered)
    mov qword ptr [rbp-56], rsi     # save arg 0 (rsi) at [rbp-56]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-64], rdx     # save arg 1 (rdx) at [rbp-64]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-72], rcx     # save arg 2 (rcx) at [rbp-72]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-80], r8      # save arg 3 (r8) at [rbp-80]
    mov qword ptr [rbp-88], r9      # save arg 4 (r9) at [rbp-88]
    
    mov r12, rdi              # fmt_ptr = format string pointer (from rdi)
    xor ebx, ebx              # arg_idx = 0
    lea r14, [rsp]            # write_buf at current rsp (bottom of allocation)

format_loop:
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r12]     # load current char
    test al, al                   # check for null terminator
    jz done_format

    cmp al, '%'
    je check_specifier
    jmp output_char

check_specifier:
    lea r13, [r12 + 1]            # point to next char after %
    movzx eax, byte ptr [r13]     # load specifier char

    cmp al, 's'
    je handle_s
    cmp al, 'd'
    je handle_d
    cmp al, 'c'
    je handle_c
    cmp al, '%'
    je handle_percent

    # Unknown specifier - output % literally and advance past it
    mov byte ptr [r14], '%'
    mov rsi, r14                  # set buffer pointer
    mov edx, 1                    # set length
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                      # advance past the first %
    jmp format_loop

output_char:
    mov byte ptr [r14], al
    mov rsi, r14                  # set buffer pointer
    mov edx, 1                    # set length
    call do_write
    add r12, 1                      # advance past literal char
    jmp format_loop

handle_s:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_s
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5 (arg indices 5+)
    mov rax, qword ptr [rbp + 8*(ebx - 3)]
    jmp load_ptr_done
fetch_reg_s:
    # Fetch from saved register slots
    mov rax, qword ptr [rbp - 56 - rbx*8]
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_ptr_done
.s_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_ptr_done
load_ptr_done:
    test rax, rax                   # check for NULL pointer
    jz .skip_s
    mov r15, rax                    # save string ptr in callee-saved reg
    xor ecx, ecx                    # count = 0
.skip_s_loop:
    cmp byte ptr [r15 + rcx], 0
    je .skip_s_done
    inc ecx
    jmp .skip_s_loop
.skip_s_done:
    mov rsi, r15                    # buf ptr for write
    mov edx, ecx                    # count for write
    call do_write
.skip_s:
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_d:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_d
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5 (arg indices 5+)
    mov rax, qword ptr [rbp + 8*(ebx - 3)]
    jmp load_int_done
fetch_reg_d:
    # Fetch from saved register slots
    mov rax, qword ptr [rbp - 56 - rbx*8]
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp load_int_done
.d_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp load_int_done
load_int_done:
    call int_to_str
    # After return: rsi = buf ptr, edx = length (set by int_to_str)
    call do_write                   # write the string
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_c:
    cmp ebx, 5
    jb fetch_reg_c_full
    # Read from stack for args beyond the first 5 (arg indices 5+)
    movzx eax, byte ptr [rbp + 8*(ebx - 3)]
    jmp done_fetch_c
fetch_reg_c_full:
    # Fetch from saved register slots
    movzx eax, byte ptr [rbp - 56 - rbx*8]
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rsi:
    mov rax, rsi
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rdx:
    mov rax, rdx
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_rcx:
    mov rax, rcx
    jmp done_fetch_c
.c_r8:
    mov rax, r8
    jmp done_fetch_c
done_fetch_c:
    mov byte ptr [r14], al          # put low byte of rax into write buffer
    mov rsi, r14                    # set buffer pointer
    mov edx, 1                      # set length
    call do_write
    add ebx, 1                      # arg_idx++
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after specifier char
    jmp format_loop

handle_percent:
    mov byte ptr [r14], '%'
    mov rsi, r14                    # set buffer pointer
    mov edx, 1                      # set length
    call do_write
    lea r12, [r13 + 1]              # fmt_ptr = after the second %
    jmp format_loop

done_format:
    # Restore callee-saved registers
    mov rbx, qword ptr [rbp-16]     # restore rbx
    mov r12, qword ptr [rbp-24]     # restore r12
    mov r13, qword ptr [rbp-32]     # restore r13
    mov r14, qword ptr [rbp-40]     # restore r14
    mov r15, qword ptr [rbp-48]     # restore r15
    mov rsp, rbp
    pop rbp
    ret

# Convert signed integer in rax to decimal string
# Output: rsi = pointer to start of string, edx = length
int_to_str:
    lea rdi, [rsp + 63]             # end of write buffer (fill right-to-left)

    mov rcx, rdi                      # current fill position
    xor ebx, ebx                      # digit count

    test rax, rax
    jns .positive

    sub rcx, 1
    mov byte ptr [rcx], '-'
    neg rax                           # negate in 64-bit (handles INT_MIN correctly)

.positive:
    test rax, rax
    jnz .digit_loop

    sub rcx, 1
    mov byte ptr [rcx], '0'
    inc ebx
    jmp .done_convert

.digit_loop:
    xor edx, edx                      # clear high bits for div
    mov r8d, 10
    div r8                            # rax = quotient, rdx = remainder

    sub rcx, 1
    add dl, '0'                       # convert digit to ASCII (dl is low byte of rdx)
    mov byte ptr [rcx], dl
    inc ebx                           # increment digit count

    test rax, rax
    jnz .digit_loop

.done_convert:
    lea rsi, [rcx + 1]                # pointer to start of string
    mov edx, ebx                      # length
    ret

# Write buffer: rsi = buf ptr, edx = byte count
# Writes to stdout (fd=1) using write(2) syscall
do_write:
    push rcx
    push r11

    mov eax, 1                        # syscall number for write
    mov edi, 1                        # fd = stdout
    syscall                           # write(1, rsi, edx)

    pop r11
    pop rcx
    ret
```

int main(void) {
    my_printf("Hello, %s! Value: %d, Negative: %d, INT_MIN: %d, Char: %c, Percent: %%\n",
              "World", 42, -17, INT_MIN, 'X');
